qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: NoSQL injection in NF Discovery endpoint enables unauthorized data extraction and query manipulation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes NoSQL injection through unsanitized query parameters reaching buildFilter() in nf_discovery.go. The advisory describes a different root cause: the EncodeGroupId function panicking due to missing bounds checks when splitting the group-id-list parameter. These are different vulnerable functions (buildFilter vs EncodeGroupId), different parameters (target-nf-type/complexQuery vs group-id-list), and different vulnerability classes (NoSQL injection vs improper input validation causing panic/DoS). Surface similarity of both affecting the NRF Discovery endpoint is insufficient for a match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes the EncodeGroupId function in the NRF discovery service panicking on malformed group-id-list parameters due to missing bounds checks on array index access ([0], [1], [2]). The sole qualifying finding (nosql-injection-nf-discovery) describes a different vulnerability: NoSQL injection through unsanitized query parameters in the buildFilter() function. These are distinct root causes in different functions with different vulnerability classes.
evidence: Advisory root cause: EncodeGroupId function accesses array indices [0], [1], [2] without length validation after splitting group-id-list parameter
Advisory impact: panic with 'index out of range' causing NRF service crash (DoS)
Finding root cause: buildFilter() in nf_discovery.go inserts user-controlled query parameter values directly into MongoDB bson.M filters without sanitization
Finding impact: unauthorized data extraction via NoSQL operator injection ($ne, $regex, etc.)
Different vulnerable functions: EncodeGroupId vs buildFilter
Different vulnerability classes: bounds-checking panic/DoS vs NoSQL injection
